adversarial_validation.perspective: Hostile reviewer attempting to break the privacy audit fix

attack_vectors.details: plistlib.load() raises InvalidFileException, caught by exception handler, returns passed=False with clear error message

attack_vectors.attack: Info.plist with valid XML but missing privacy keys
attack_vectors.details: Parsed plist correctly identifies missing keys, returns appropriate failure message
plistlib.load() handles both XML and binary plist formats, works correctly
UnicodeDecodeError caught, file skipped gracefully, continues with other files

attack_vectors.attack: Swift file with embedded null bytes
attack_vectors.details: Exception caught, file skipped, error reported in unreadable_files list
open() raises exception, caught by exception handler, returns clear error
plistlib.load() processes file, may be slow but doesn't crash. Production should add size limit.
read_text() handles large files, performance degradation acceptable for pre-submission testing

adversarial_validation.recommendation: FIX-001a is robust against common attack vectors. Consider adding file size limits in production.

attack_vectors.details: Each file read independently, encoding errors caught per-file, continues processing
Returns passed=True with details showing 0 files checked. User gets clear feedback.
Exception caught, file added to unreadable list, continues with remaining files

bug_id_002.recommendation: FIX-002a handles encoding errors gracefully. Per-file exception handling prevents cascade failures.

attack_vectors.details: Falls back to 'MyApp' or first 3 words of description
strip() removes whitespace, triggers fallback logic
Truncated to 30 chars, word boundary respected
